entries.title: Functional-only learning app provides no retention hook
entries.description: A learning app that correctly displays questions and answers but lacks emotional engagement, progress visibility, and motivational feedback will not retain users. Functional correctness is necessary but insufficient for a product users return to voluntarily.
entries.context: AskFin prototype provided accurate question analysis and learning modes but felt like a tool, not a coach. Users had no reason to return daily without external motivation.
entries.lesson: Define the emotional core of the product before building features. Ask: why would a user open this tomorrow without being told to?

entries.title: Emotional micro-copy outperforms data-only feedback
entries.description: Contextual, emotionally aware feedback text drives stronger user engagement than raw data display. 'Stark! 8 von 10 richtig' outperforms 'Score: 80%'. Feedback after correct answers should differ from feedback after incorrect answers.
entries.context: Identified during AskFin premium reframing. Generic score displays were emotionally flat. Reframing to contextual micro-copy with personality made the same data feel like coaching.

entries.title: Domain-specific progress tracking beats generic gamification
entries.description: A progress metric tied to the user's real goal (e.g. exam readiness score 0-100%) is more motivating than generic points, badges, or XP systems. Users care about 'Am I ready for my exam?' not 'How many points do I have?'.
entries.context: AskFin reframing replaced abstract score tracking with a Pruefungsbereitschafts-Score (exam readiness percentage) and a Skill Map showing topic-level competence. This reframing changed the product from a question tool to a coaching system.
entries.effect: Transforms utility tool into goal-oriented coaching experience. User sees meaningful progress toward their actual objective.

entries.title: Reset SelectorGroupChat between pipeline passes to prevent context explosion
entries.description: AutoGen SelectorGroupChat accumulates all messages across team.run() calls in _message_thread, _message_buffer, and agent _model_context. After a heavy pass (25-40 generated files), accumulated context exceeds Haiku's 50k input token/min rate limit. Calling team.reset() between passes clears all accumulated state.
entries.context: DriveAI pipeline crashed consistently at Bug Hunter Pass (first pass after Implementation) with anthropic.RateLimitError 429. Root cause: 60-80k tokens accumulated from Implementation Pass sent to Haiku in a single request.

entries.title: Compact implementation summary restores review quality after context reset
entries.description: After team.reset() clears context between passes, review agents lose all implementation detail. A deterministic summary (~300-2000 chars) built from extraction metadata (file names, type keywords, architectural patterns) restores enough context for file-specific reviews without reintroducing token pressure.
entries.context: After implementing FK-004 (context reset), Bug Hunter and Refactor passes gave generic feedback disconnected from generated code. Adding a compact summary prepended to review task prompts restored file-specific bug detection and refactoring suggestions.

entries.title: New review agents should start as advisory-only passes
entries.description: When introducing a new review agent (e.g. Creative Director), start it as advisory-only with strict message limits (MaxMessageTermination=2) and no veto power. This validates the agent's value without risking pipeline stability. Promote to blocking gate only after the agent consistently produces actionable feedback.
entries.context: Creative Director was introduced as a separate review pass after Bug Hunter, advisory only, with MaxMessageTermination(2). This prevented runaway conversations and allowed safe validation. The CD produced useful product-level feedback without destabilizing the pipeline.
